fact: July 29 is exactly 210 days into the year - that's 30 weeks of pure summer vibes!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when cicadas are typically screaming their loudest - some make sounds as loud as a motorcycle!
In Australia, penguins are usually huddling together in the freezing winter cold while the Northern Hemisphere is experiencing summer heat!
The sun sets almost 2 minutes earlier today than it did on July 1st - summer is already sneaking away!
In northern Canada, the sun still doesn't set until after 9 PM, giving kids there super long days to play outside!
This is typically peak butterfly season in most temperate regions - monarch butterflies are often getting ready for their epic 3,000-mile journey south!
July 29 falls exactly 5 months and 8 days after the spring equinox - nature's been growing like crazy this whole time!
In many places, this is when fireflies often start to disappear for the year - their magical light show is almost over!
Ocean water in the Northern Hemisphere typically reaches some of its warmest temperatures right around now - perfect for swimming!
In the Southern Hemisphere, ski resorts are usually packed with powder because it's the middle of winter - imagine building snowmen while the north has pool parties!
This date is exactly 148 days away from Christmas - that's about 21 weeks of waiting!
